Here's the Kotlin code to solve the problem:

```kotlin
fun solve(nums: Array<Long>, k: Long): Long {
    val prefixSum = mutableMapOf<Long, Long>()
    prefixSum[0L] = -1L
    var sum = 0L
    var minSum = 0L
    var totalSum = 0L
    var lastIndex = -1

    for (i in nums.indices) {
        totalSum += nums[i]
        sum += nums[i]
        val mod = (sum % k + k) % k
        if (prefixSum.containsKey(mod)) {
            val prevIndex = prefixSum[mod]!!
            if (prevIndex >= lastIndex) {
                minSum += sum - (if (prevIndex == -1L) 0 else sum - nums[i])
                sum = 0L
                lastIndex = i
            }
        }
        prefixSum[mod] = i.toLong()
    }

    return totalSum - minSum
}
```

This solution uses a prefix sum approach with a hash map to track remainders. The key idea is to find contiguous subarrays whose sum is divisible by k and remove them to minimize the remaining sum. The algorithm keeps track of prefix sums and their remainders when divided by k, allowing it to efficiently identify removable subarrays.

The function calculates the total sum of the array and subtracts the sum of all removable subarrays to get the minimum possible remaining sum. The approach is efficient with O(n) time complexity, which is important given the constraints (nums.length up to 10^5).

```kotlin
fun solve(nums: Array<Long>, k: Long): Long {
    val prefixSum = mutableMapOf<Long, Long>()
    prefixSum[0L] = -1L
    var sum = 0L
    var minSum = 0L
    var totalSum = 0L
    var result = 0L

    for (i in nums.indices) {
        totalSum += nums[i]
        sum += nums[i]
        val mod = (sum % k + k) % k
        if (prefixSum.containsKey(mod)) {
            val prevIndex = prefixSum[mod]!!
            if (prevIndex != -2L) {
                minSum = sum - (sum - (prevIndex + 1).let { if (it >= 0) nums[it.toInt()] else 0L })
                sum = minSum
                prefixSum.clear()
                prefixSum[0L] = -1L
            }
        }
        prefixSum[mod] = i.toLong()
    }
    result = sum
    return result
}
```

```kotlin
fun solve(nums: Array<Long>, k: Long): Long {
    var sum = 0L
    val modMap = mutableMapOf<Long, Long>()
    modMap[0] = -1
    var minSum = 0L
    var currentMod = 0L
    var lastIndex = -1

    for (i in nums.indices) {
        sum += nums[i]
        currentMod = (sum % k + k) % k
        if (modMap.containsKey(currentMod)) {
            val prevIndex = modMap[currentMod]!!
            if (prevIndex >= lastIndex) {
                minSum += sum - (if (prevIndex == -1L) 0 else sum - nums[i])
                lastIndex = i
                sum = 0
                modMap.clear()
                modMap[0] = -1
                currentMod = 0
                continue
            }
        }
        modMap[currentMod] = i.toLong()
    }
    return sum + minSum
}
```
